Originally, it was the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}}'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden}} is surrounded by {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|Tamagaki=Tamagaki|lt=stone walls}}, as seen in the photo. There is no {{ill|offering box|ja|賽銭}}. It is located approximately 50 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m}} deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai}} reported to the {{ill|Ministry of Shinto|ja|神祇省}} that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社}} in {{ill|Dokaigun|ja|度会郡}} Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:Intermediate and Subsidiary Shrines]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}末社 |創建= {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}以前 |本殿= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= {{ill|Shinano-sai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}{{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|deity|ja|祭神}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}}'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden}} is surrounded by {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|Tamagaki=Tamagaki|lt=stone walls}}, as seen in the photo. There is no {{ill|offering box|ja|賽銭}}. It is located approximately 50 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m}} deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai}} reported to the {{ill|Ministry of Shinto|ja|神祇省}} that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社}} in {{ill|Dokaigun|ja|度会郡}} Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:Intermediate and Subsidiary Shrines]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:New]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:Intermediate and Subsidiary Shrines]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:New]]

As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai}} reported to the {{ill|Ministry of Shinto|ja|神祇省}} that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社}} in {{ill|Dokaigun|ja|度会郡}} Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}末社 |創建= {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}以前 |本殿= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= {{ill|Shinano-sai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}{{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|deity|ja|祭神}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}}'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden}} is surrounded by {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|Tamagaki=Tamagaki|lt=stone walls}}, as seen in the photo. There is no {{ill|offering box|ja|賽銭}}. It is located approximately 50 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m}} deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai}} reported to the {{ill|Ministry of Shinto|ja|神祇省}} that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社}} in {{ill|Dokaigun|ja|度会郡}} Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}末社 |創建= {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}以前 |本殿= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= {{ill|Shinano-sai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}{{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|deity|ja|祭神}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}}'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden}} is surrounded by {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|Tamagaki=Tamagaki|lt=stone walls}}, as seen in the photo. There is no {{ill|offering box|ja|賽銭}}. It is located approximately 50 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m}} deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai}} reported to the {{ill|Ministry of Shinto|ja|神祇省}} that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社}} in {{ill|Dokaigun|ja|度会郡}} Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:Intermediate and Subsidiary Shrines]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}末社 |創建= {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}以前 |本殿= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= {{ill|Shinano-sai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}{{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|deity|ja|祭神}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}}'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden}} is surrounded by {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|Tamagaki=Tamagaki|lt=stone walls}}, as seen in the photo. There is no {{ill|offering box|ja|賽銭}}. It is located approximately 50 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m}} deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai}} reported to the {{ill|Ministry of Shinto|ja|神祇省}} that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社}} in {{ill|Dokaigun|ja|度会郡}} Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:Intermediate and Subsidiary Shrines]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. revision.comment: Text replacement - "|神宮125社の一覧" to "|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}末社 |創建= {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}以前 |本殿= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= {{ill|Shinano-sai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}{{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|deity|ja|祭神}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}}'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden}} is surrounded by {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|Tamagaki=Tamagaki|lt=stone walls}}, as seen in the photo. There is no {{ill|offering box|ja|賽銭}}. It is located approximately 50 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m}} deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai}} reported to the {{ill|Ministry of Shinto|ja|神祇省}} that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社}} in {{ill|Dokaigun|ja|度会郡}} Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:Intermediate and Subsidiary Shrines]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:Intermediate and Subsidiary Shrines]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai}} reported to the {{ill|Ministry of Shinto|ja|神祇省}} that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社}} in {{ill|Dokaigun|ja|度会郡}} Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}末社 |創建= {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}以前 |本殿= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= {{ill|Shinano-sai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}{{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|deity|ja|祭神}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}}'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden}} is surrounded by {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|Tamagaki=Tamagaki|lt=stone walls}}, as seen in the photo. There is no {{ill|offering box|ja|賽銭}}. It is located approximately 50 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m}} deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,

Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:Intermediate and Subsidiary Shrines]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}末社 |創建= {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}以前 |本殿= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= {{ill|Shinano-sai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}{{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|deity|ja|祭神}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}}'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden}} is surrounded by {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|Tamagaki=Tamagaki|lt=stone walls}}, as seen in the photo. There is no {{ill|offering box|ja|賽銭}}. It is located approximately 50 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m}} deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai}} reported to the {{ill|Ministry of Shinto|ja|神祇省}} that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社}} in {{ill|Dokaigun|ja|度会郡}} Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Intermediate and Subsidiary Shrines]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}}
