The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture|12=draft}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄|12=draft}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線|12=draft}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|12=draft}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|12=draft}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=draft}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|12=draft}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=draft}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Intermediate and Subsidiary Shrines]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|12=draft}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}}末社 |創建= {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}以前 |本殿= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= {{ill|Shinano-sai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}{{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=draft}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|12=draft}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|12=draft}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|12=draft}}'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港|12=draft}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden|12=draft}} is surrounded by {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|Tamagaki=Tamagaki|lt=stone walls|12=draft}}, as seen in the photo. There is no {{ill|offering box|ja|賽銭|12=draft}}. It is located approximately 50 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m|12=draft}} deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=draft}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō|12=draft}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|12=draft}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|12=draft}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=draft}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4th year|12=draft}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|12=draft}} reported to the {{ill|Ministry of Shinto|ja|神祇省|12=draft}} that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Dokaigun|ja|度会郡|12=draft}} Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}} {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|12=draft}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|12=draft}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture|12=draft}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄|12=draft}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線|12=draft}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|12=draft}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|12=draft}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=draft}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|12=draft}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=draft}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Intermediate and Subsidiary Shrines]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)}}

Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft|12=draft}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線|12=draft|12=draft}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|12=draft|12=draft}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|12=draft|12=draft}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft|12=draft}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=draft|12=draft}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|12=draft|12=draft}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=draft|12=draft}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Intermediate and Subsidiary Shrines]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|教部省|" to "|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|12=draft|12=draft}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}末社 |創建= {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}以前 |本殿= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= {{ill|Shinano-sai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=draft|12=draft}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=draft|12=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=draft|12=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|12=draft|12=draft}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|12=draft|12=draft}}'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden|12=draft|12=draft}} is surrounded by {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|Tamagaki=Tamagaki|lt=stone walls|12=draft|12=draft}}, as seen in the photo. There is no {{ill|offering box|ja|賽銭|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is located approximately 50 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m|12=draft|12=draft}} deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō|12=draft|12=draft}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|12=draft|12=draft}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=draft|12=draft}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|12=draft|12=draft}} reported to the {{ill|Ministry of Shinto|ja|神祇省|12=draft|12=draft}} that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Dokaigun|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}} Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|12=draft|12=draft}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft|12=draft}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft|12=draft}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft|12=draft}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture|12=draft|12=draft}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft|12=draft}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft|12=draft}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft|12=draft}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線|12=draft|12=draft}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|12=draft|12=draft}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|12=draft|12=draft}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft|12=draft}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=draft|12=draft}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|12=draft|12=draft}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=draft|12=draft}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Intermediate and Subsidiary Shrines]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)}}

　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Intermediate and Subsidiary Shrines]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)}}

revision.comment: 14 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大津神社 (伊勢市) up to rev 63996440
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 大津神社 |画像= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]] |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= [[豊受大神宮]]末社 |創建= [[平安時代]]以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''大津神社'''（おおつじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[豊受大神宮]]（外宮）の末社。外宮の宮域内にあり、北御門脇の小道沿いにある[[度会国御神社]]のさらに奥へ進んだところに鎮座する[[アイティービー|iTV]]"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php 度会国御神社|伊勢神宮百科辞典|伊勢志摩の旅よいとこせ]"（2012年1月7日閲覧。），iTV"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php 大津神社|伊勢神宮百科辞典|伊勢志摩の旅よいとこせ]"（2012年1月7日閲覧。）。 == 概要 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]]の伊勢神宮外宮境内に鎮座する。外宮の末社8社のうち第7位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：15ページ。 [[祭神]]は葦原神（あしはらのかみ）伊勢文化舎（2008）：37ページ。もとは[[五十鈴川]]の河口にある[[港町]]の[[神社港|神社]]（かみやしろ）や[[大湊 (伊勢市)|大湊]]の[[守護神]]である。 [[社殿]]は、写真にあるように[[玉垣]]に囲まれている。[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。度会国御神社からおよそ50[[メートル|m]]奥に入ったところに鎮座する。大津神社の更に先には、外宮所管社の[[上御井神社]]（かみのみいのじんじゃ）が鎮座するが、立ち入ることはできない。摂社および末社は神宮式年遷宮の対象とならない櫻井（1991）：88ページため、20年に一度の建て替えは行われない。 == 歴史 == 創建は未詳。伊勢神宮外宮の末社は『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載されず当時は国家の[[祭祀]]を受けていなかったが、[[平安時代]]の初期には度会宮の所管として公認されていた神社であることから、平安時代以前には存在したと考えられる。 その後一時期、[[行方不明]]となり祭祀が断絶するiTV"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php 大津神社|伊勢神宮百科辞典|伊勢志摩の旅よいとこせ]"（2012年1月7日閲覧。）。[[明治4年]]（[[1871年]]）、[[度会県]]は[[神祇省]]に[[度会郡]]王中島村（現・伊勢市御薗町王中島）の足穂神社が外宮末社の大津神社であると届け出た櫻井（1991）：275ページ。この届出は住民に無断で行われたと見られ、[[神宮司庁]]が王中島村の惣代に足穂神社を大津神社に改称するよう交渉したところ争論となった。以後、度会県・神宮司庁・村方の三者で折衝が続けられ、「村内にあるのは足穂社と足穂社境内社の大水社のみであり、大津社の名は古老も聞いたことがない」という村方の主張が認められた櫻井（1991）：275 - 276ページ。神宮司庁側に足穂社を大津社だと断定できるだけの証拠を有していなかったことも村方の意見が受容された要因の1つである。神宮司庁は[[教部省]]に対応を諮り、[[1873年]]（明治6年）8月に外宮宮域内に新たに社殿を設けるよう通達した櫻井（1991）：276ページ。神宮司庁は同年中に現在地に社殿を建設、大津神社の祭祀を再興した。[[櫻井勝之進]]は大津神社再興に関して、以下のように私的見解を述べている。 {{Quotation|管見によれば、明治三年の「外宮交名帳」（久志本本）には、
　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
とあるから、足穂社でなく同域内の大水社をこそ末社と指定すべきであった。 |櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』276ページ}} 社殿は[[1923年]]（大正12年）3月に建て替えが行われた。 == 交通 == ;公共交通 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]]伊勢市駅南口（JR）より徒歩5分程度伊勢文化舎（2008）：33ページ。 * [[三重交通]]「伊勢市駅前」バス停より徒歩5分。 * 三重交通「外宮前」バス停よりすぐ。 ;自家用車 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を北へ約5分。北御門前に434台収容の[[無料]][[駐車場]]あり神宮司庁"[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html 駐車場案内]"（2011年1月8日閲覧。）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == 関連項目 == * [[神宮125社の一覧]] * [[大津神社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html 大津神社（おおつじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:おおつしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:摂末社]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[大津神社 (伊勢市)]] to [[Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:

　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
とあるから、足穂社でなく同域内の大水社をこそ末社と指定すべきであった。 |櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』276ページ}} 社殿は[[1923年]]（大正12年）3月に建て替えが行われた。 == 交通 == ;公共交通 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]]伊勢市駅南口（JR）より徒歩5分程度伊勢文化舎（2008）：33ページ。 * [[三重交通]]「伊勢市駅前」バス停より徒歩5分。 * 三重交通「外宮前」バス停よりすぐ。 ;自家用車 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を北へ約5分。北御門前に434台収容の[[無料]][[駐車場]]あり神宮司庁"[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html 駐車場案内]"（2011年1月8日閲覧。）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == 関連項目 == * [[神宮125社の一覧]] * [[大津神社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html 大津神社（おおつじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:おおつしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:摂末社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}末社 |創建= {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}以前 |本殿= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= {{ill|Shinano-sai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Toyouke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is surrounded by {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|Tamagaki=Tamagaki|lt=stone walls|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as seen in the photo. There is no {{ill|offering box|ja|賽銭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is located approximately 50 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m|qq=draft|qq=draft}} deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reported to the {{ill|Ministry of Shinto|ja|神祇省|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Dokaigun|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}末社 |創建= {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}以前 |本殿= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= {{ill|Shinano-sai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is surrounded by {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|Tamagaki=Tamagaki|lt=stone walls|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as seen in the photo. There is no {{ill|offering box|ja|賽銭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is located approximately 50 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m|qq=draft|qq=draft}} deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reported to the {{ill|Ministry of Shinto|ja|神祇省|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Dokaigun|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)}} {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|version=63996440|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Intermediate and Subsidiary Shrines]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}末社 |創建= {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}以前 |本殿= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= {{ill|Shinano-sai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is surrounded by {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|Tamagaki=Tamagaki|lt=stone walls|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as seen in the photo. There is no {{ill|offering box|ja|賽銭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is located approximately 50 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m|qq=draft|qq=draft}} deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reported to the {{ill|Ministry of Shinto|ja|神祇省|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Dokaigun|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)}} {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|version=63996440|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Intermediate and Subsidiary Shrines]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]]

In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reported to the {{ill|Ministry of Shinto|ja|神祇省|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Dokaigun|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)}} {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|version=63996440|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Intermediate and Subsidiary Shrines]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}末社 |創建= {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}以前 |本殿= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= {{ill|Shinano-sai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is surrounded by {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|Tamagaki=Tamagaki|lt=stone walls|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as seen in the photo. There is no {{ill|offering box|ja|賽銭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is located approximately 50 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m|qq=draft|qq=draft}} deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reported to the {{ill|Ministry of Shinto|ja|神祇省|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Dokaigun|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}末社 |創建= {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}以前 |本殿= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= {{ill|Shinano-sai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is surrounded by {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|Tamagaki=Tamagaki|lt=stone walls|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as seen in the photo. There is no {{ill|offering box|ja|賽銭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is located approximately 50 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m|qq=draft|qq=draft}} deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reported to the {{ill|Ministry of Shinto|ja|神祇省|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Dokaigun|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, December 23, 2008, p.151.
