However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/missing|qq=}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 4|qq=}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|qq=}} reported to the {{ill|Ministry of Shinto|ja|神祇省|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Shinto|qq=}} that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiho Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Dokaigun|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokaigun|qq=}} Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City|qq=}} {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|qq=}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Religion (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Religion (Japan)|qq=}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu|qq=}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Yamada Line|qq=}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|qq=}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|qq=}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/parking lot|qq=}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka-sha|qq=}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Publishing|qq=}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|version=63996440|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City|qq=}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|qq=}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}}末社 |創建= {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}以前 |本殿= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= {{ill|Shinano-sai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano-sai|qq=}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokaikoku Shrine|qq=}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City|qq=}}{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|qq=}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu River|qq=}}'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Port Town|qq=}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Port|qq=}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomomachi (Ise City)|qq=}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjugami|qq=}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden|qq=}} is surrounded by {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|Tamagaki=Tamagaki|lt=stone walls|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagaki|qq=}}, as seen in the photo. There is no {{ill|offering box|ja|賽銭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering box|qq=}}. It is located approximately 50 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/meter|qq=}} deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|qq=}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/missing|qq=}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 4|qq=}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|qq=}} reported to the {{ill|Ministry of Shinto|ja|神祇省|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Shinto|qq=}} that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiho Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Dokaigun|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokaigun|qq=}} Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City|qq=}} {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|qq=}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} consulted with the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|Ministry of Religion (Japan)=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Education and Culture|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Religion (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Religion (Japan)|qq=}} and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}}・{{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|lt=|ltja=近鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu|qq=}} {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|ltja=山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Yamada Line|qq=}} Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}} at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|qq=}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|qq=}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/parking lot|qq=}} with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka-sha|qq=}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Publishing|qq=}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|version=63996440|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]]{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|qq=}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}}末社 |創建= [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]以前 |本殿= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Shinano-sai]] }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokaikoku Shrine|qq=}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]]{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|qq=}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Port Town|qq=}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Port|qq=}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomomachi (Ise City)|qq=}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjugami|qq=}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden|qq=}} is surrounded by [[:en:Tamagaki|stone walls]], as seen in the photo. There is no [[:en:Saisen|offering box]]. It is located approximately 50 [[:en:Metre|m]] deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|qq=}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/missing|qq=}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 4|qq=}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|qq=}} reported to the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Shinto]] that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiho Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Dokaigun]] Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|qq=}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} consulted with the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Education and Culture]] and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]・[[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|qq=}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|qq=}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free [[:en:Parking lot|parking lot]] with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka-sha|qq=}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Publishing|qq=}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|version=63996440|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]]

The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} consulted with the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Education and Culture]] and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]・[[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|qq=}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|qq=}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free [[:en:Parking lot|parking lot]] with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka-sha|qq=}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Publishing|qq=}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|version=63996440|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]]{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|qq=}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}}末社 |創建= [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]以前 |本殿= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Shinano-sai]] }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokaikoku Shrine|qq=}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]]{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|qq=}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Port Town|qq=}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Port|qq=}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomomachi (Ise City)|qq=}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjugami|qq=}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden|qq=}} is surrounded by [[:en:Tamagaki|stone walls]], as seen in the photo. There is no [[:en:Saisen|offering box]]. It is located approximately 50 [[:en:Metre|m]] deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|qq=}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/missing|qq=}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 4|qq=}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|qq=}} reported to the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Shinto]] that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiho Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Dokaigun]] Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|qq=}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} consulted with the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Education and Culture]] and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]・[[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|qq=}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|qq=}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free [[:en:Parking lot|parking lot]] with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka-sha|qq=}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Publishing|qq=}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|version=63996440|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[ja:大津神社 (伊勢市)]]

The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}} consulted with the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Education and Culture]] and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]・[[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|qq=}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|qq=}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free [[:en:Parking lot|parking lot]] with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka-sha|qq=}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Publishing|qq=}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|version=63996440|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[ja:大津神社 (伊勢市)]]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] into [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] via bot
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]]{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|qq=}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建= [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]以前 |本殿= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Shinano-sai]] }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokaikoku Shrine|qq=}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]]{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|qq=}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Port Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港 (曖昧さ回避)}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Port|qq=}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomomachi (Ise City)|qq=}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjugami|qq=}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}} is surrounded by [[:en:Tamagaki|stone walls]], as seen in the photo. There is no [[:en:Saisen|offering box]]. It is located approximately 50 [[:en:Metre|m]] deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/missing|qq=}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1871年}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|qq=}} reported to the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Shinto]] that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiho Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Dokaigun]] Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}} consulted with the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Education and Culture]] and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]・[[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|qq=}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|qq=}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free [[:en:Parking lot|parking lot]] with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka-sha|qq=}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Publishing|qq=}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|version=63996440|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[ja:大津神社 (伊勢市)]]

As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/missing|qq=}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1871年}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|qq=}} reported to the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Shinto]] that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiho Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Dokaigun]] Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}} consulted with the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Education and Culture]] and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]・[[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|qq=}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|qq=}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free [[:en:Parking lot|parking lot]] with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka-sha|qq=}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Publishing|qq=}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|version=63996440|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E4%BC%8A%E5%8B%A2%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:大津神社]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[ja:大津神社 (伊勢市)]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]]{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|qq=}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建= [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]以前 |本殿= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Shinano-sai]] }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokaikoku Shrine|qq=}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]]{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|qq=}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Port Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港 (曖昧さ回避)}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Port|qq=}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomomachi (Ise City)|qq=}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjugami|qq=}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}} is surrounded by [[:en:Tamagaki|stone walls]], as seen in the photo. There is no [[:en:Saisen|offering box]]. It is located approximately 50 [[:en:Metre|m]] deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/missing|qq=}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1871年}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|qq=}} reported to the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Shinto]] that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiho Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Dokaigun]] Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}} consulted with the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Education and Culture]] and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]・[[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|qq=}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|qq=}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free [[:en:Parking lot|parking lot]] with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka-sha|qq=}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, April 25, 1998, p.237.
