{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11437359]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]]{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|qq=|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建= [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]以前 |本殿= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Shinano-sai]] }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokaikoku Shrine|qq=}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]]{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|qq=|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Port Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Port Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3272531}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Port|qq=|1=Kamiyashirokō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226485}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomomachi (Ise City)|qq=|1=Oomomachi (Ise City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11437521}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjugami|qq=}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is surrounded by [[:en:Tamagaki|stone walls]], as seen in the photo. There is no [[:en:Saisen|offering box]]. It is located approximately 50 [[:en:Metre|m]] deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/missing|qq=|1=missing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11285787}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1871年|1=1871|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7746}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|qq=|1=Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}} reported to the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Shinto]] that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiho Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Dokaigun]] Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|qq=|1=Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} consulted with the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Education and Culture]] and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]・[[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|qq=|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|qq=|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free [[:en:Parking lot|parking lot]] with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka-sha|qq=|1=Ise Bunka-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379080}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Publishing|qq=|1=Kokusho Kankōkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420946}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. [[ja:大津神社 (伊勢市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11437359]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]]{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|qq=|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建= [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]以前 |本殿= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Shinano-sai]] }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokaikoku Shrine|qq=}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]]{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|qq=|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Port Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Port Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3272531}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Port|qq=|1=Kamiyashirokō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226485}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomomachi (Ise City)|qq=|1=Oomomachi (Ise City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11437521}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjugami|qq=}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is surrounded by [[:en:Tamagaki|stone walls]], as seen in the photo. There is no [[:en:Saisen|offering box]]. It is located approximately 50 [[:en:Metre|m]] deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/missing|qq=|1=missing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11285787}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|qq=|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1871年|1=1871|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7746}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|qq=|1=Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}} reported to the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Shinto]] that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiho Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Dokaigun]] Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|qq=|1=Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} consulted with the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Education and Culture]] and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|qq=|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]・[[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|qq=|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|qq=|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free [[:en:Parking lot|parking lot]] with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka-sha|qq=|1=Ise Bunka-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379080}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Publishing|qq=|1=Kokusho Kankōkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420946}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokaikoku Shrine|qq=}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]]{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Port Town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Port Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3272531}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Port|1=Kamiyashirokō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226485}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomomachi (Ise City)|1=Oomomachi (Ise City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11437521}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjugami|qq=}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is surrounded by [[:en:Tamagaki|stone walls]], as seen in the photo. There is no [[:en:Saisen|offering box]]. It is located approximately 50 [[:en:Metre|m]] deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/missing|1=missing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11285787}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 4|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1871年|1=1871|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7746}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|1=Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}} reported to the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Shinto]] that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiho Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Dokaigun]] Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|1=Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} consulted with the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Education and Culture]] and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]・[[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free [[:en:Parking lot|parking lot]] with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka-sha|1=Ise Bunka-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379080}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Publishing|1=Kokusho Kankōkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420946}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 4|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1871年|1=1871|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7746}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|1=Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}} reported to the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Shinto]] that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiho Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Dokaigun]] Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} consulted with the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Education and Culture]] and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]・[[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free [[:en:Parking lot|parking lot]] with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka-sha|1=Ise Bunka-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379080}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Publishing|1=Kokusho Kankōkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420946}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|version=63996440|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:大津神社]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[ja:大津神社 (伊勢市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17222697}}

[[ja:大津神社 (伊勢市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11437359]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]]{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建= [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]以前 |本殿= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Shinano-sai]] }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokaikoku Shrine|qq=}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]]{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Port Town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Port Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3272531}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Port|1=Kamiyashirokō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226485}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomomachi (Ise City)|1=Oomomachi (Ise City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11437521}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjugami|qq=}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is surrounded by [[:en:Tamagaki|stone walls]], as seen in the photo. There is no [[:en:Saisen|offering box]]. It is located approximately 50 [[:en:Metre|m]] deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/missing|1=missing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11285787}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 4|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1871年|1=1871|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7746}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|1=Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}} reported to the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Shinto]] that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiho Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Dokaigun]] Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|1=Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} consulted with the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Education and Culture]] and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]・[[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free [[:en:Parking lot|parking lot]] with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka-sha|1=Ise Bunka-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379080}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Publishing|1=Kokusho Kankōkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420946}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|version=63996440|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:大津神社]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[ja:大津神社 (伊勢市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17222697}}

It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokaikoku Shrine|qq=}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]]{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Port Town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Port Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3272531}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Port|1=Kamiyashirokō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226485}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomomachi (Ise City)|1=Oomomachi (Ise City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11437521}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjugami|qq=}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is surrounded by [[:en:Tamagaki|stone walls]], as seen in the photo. There is no [[:en:Saisen|offering box]]. It is located approximately 50 [[:en:Metre|m]] deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/missing|1=missing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11285787}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 4|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1871年|1=1871|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7746}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|1=Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}} reported to the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Shinto]] that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiho Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Dokaigun]] Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|1=Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} consulted with the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Education and Culture]] and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]・[[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free [[:en:Parking lot|parking lot]] with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka-sha|1=Ise Bunka-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379080}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Publishing|1=Kokusho Kankōkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420946}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine 』, {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, April 25, 1998, p.237. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == Related Items == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0205.html Ootsu Shrine (Ootsu Jinja)] - Ise Grand Shrine Worship Association {{Jingu 125 Shrines}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|version=63996440|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:大津神社]] [[ja:Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[ja:大津神社 (伊勢市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17222697}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:大津神社 (伊勢市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11437359]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称= 大津神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ōtsu-jinja (Geku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]]{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 19.7 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 |経度秒 = 5.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 葦原神 |社格= {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建= [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]以前 |本殿= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Shinano-sai]] }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Outer Shrine)'s subsidiary shrine. It is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine and sits further into the area beyond the {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Watarai Kunimi Shrine|lt=Dokaikoku Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokaikoku Shrine|qq=}} along the small path beside the North Omikado gate.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/w0001.php Dokaikoku Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.), {{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.). == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]]{{ill|Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa Town (Ise City)|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}'s Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine precinct houses Ootsu Shrine. It is the 7th among the 8 subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall edited (1929): p.15. {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} is Ashihara-no-Kami (葦原神)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.37. Originally, it was the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]'s estuary's {{ill|Port Town|ja|港町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Port Town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Port Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3272531}}'s {{ill|Shrine Port|ja|神社港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Port|1=Kamiyashirokō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226485}} and {{ill|Oomomachi (Ise City)|ja|大湊|lt=Oomomachi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomomachi (Ise City)|1=Oomomachi (Ise City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11437521}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|守護神|lt=Guardian deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjugami|qq=}}. The {{ill|Shaden|ja|社殿|Shaden=Shaden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is surrounded by [[:en:Tamagaki|stone walls]], as seen in the photo. There is no [[:en:Saisen|offering box]]. It is located approximately 50 [[:en:Metre|m]] deep into the area from Dokaikoku Shrine.. Further ahead of Ootsu Shrine, the Outer Shrine's jurisdiction's {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} (かみのみいのじんじゃ) is located, but it is not accessible.. As the intermediate and subsidiary shrines are not subjects of the Ise Shrine's Sengu (Shrine's renewal ceremony), they are not rebuilt every 20 years.Sakurai (1991): p.88. == History == The founding date is unknown. The subsidiary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine were not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|Engishiki=Engishiki|lt=Engishiki Jinmei-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, and at that time, they did not receive national {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}}. However, since it was an officially recognized shrine under the jurisdiction of the Dokaikami in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it is believed to have existed before the Heian period.. Subsequently, it became {{ill|missing|ja|行方不明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/missing|1=missing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11285787}} for a period, and the rituals were discontinued.{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}}"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/a0008.php Ootsu Shrine|Ise Shrine Encyclopedia Dictionary|Ise-Shima Travel Good Spots]" (accessed January 7, 2012.) In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 4|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1871年|1=1871|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7746}} (1871), {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|1=Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}} reported to the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Shinto]] that the {{ill|Ashiho Shrine|ja|足穂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiho Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Dokaigun]] Ōnakanoshima Village (present-day [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] {{ill|Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja|御薗町王中島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misonocho Ōnakanoshima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was the Ootsu Shrine of the Outer Shrine's subsidiary shrines.Sakurai (1991): p.275 This notification was made without the consent of the residents, and when the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} negotiated with the village leader to rename Ashiho Shrine to Ootsu Shrine, a dispute arose. Since then, negotiations continued among {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|1=Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}}, {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}}, and the village side. The village side's claim that "within the village, there are only Ashiho Shrine and Ōmizu Shrine, the shrine within the precincts of Ashiho Shrine, and no one has heard of a shrine named Ootsu-sha" was accepted.Sakurai (1991): pp.275 - 276 One of the reasons for accepting the village's opinion was that the {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} did not have sufficient evidence to definitively identify Ashiho Shrine as Ootsu Shrine. The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} consulted with the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Education and Culture]] and notified in August 1873 (Meiji 6) to establish new shrine buildings within the Outer Shrine's precincts.Sakurai (1991): p.276 The {{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} built the shrine buildings at the current location within the same year and revived the rituals of Ootsu Shrine. {{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} expressed the following private opinion regarding the revival of Ootsu Shrine:. {{Quotation|In my opinion, in the "Outer Shrine name Register" of Meiji 3 (Kushi-hon), it states,
　　大津社一名大水社社地　王中島村　村人造替
Therefore, instead of Ashiho Shrine, Ōmizu Shrine within the same area should have been designated as the subsidiary shrine. |{{ill|Sakurai Katsuyuki|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsuyuki|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}}『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』 p.276}} The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taisho 12).. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]・[[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station South Exit (JR) is about a 5-minute walk.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Ise City Station Front" bus stop is about a 5-minute walk. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop is right there. ; Private Vehicle * From [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] at {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|lt=Ise West Interchange|lt2=伊勢西IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}}, go north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road 32 Ise Isobe Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mokimochi Road) for about 5 minutes. There is a free [[:en:Parking lot|parking lot]] with a capacity of 434 vehicles in front of the North Omikado gate.{{ill|Jingu Shrine Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed January 8, 2011.). == Footnotes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * 'O Ise-san 125-sha Meguri' special issue 'Isejin', {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka-sha|1=Ise Bunka-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379080}}, December 23, 2008, p.151. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Hall 『Ujiyamada City History Volume 1』, Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, Showa 4, p.862. * Sakurai Katsuyuki 『Ise Grand Shrine's Ancestral Model and Development』, {{ill|Kokusho Publishing|ja|国書刊行会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Publishing|1=Kokusho Kankōkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420946}}, November 30, Heisei 3, p.318.
